pages.text:
Foreword
For many years, guinea pigs were afforded only a single chapter in the 
BSAVA Manual of Exotic Pets, alongside chinchillas, degus and duprasi. 
Then, in 2009, they got an increased billing as a contributing species in 
the BSAVA Manual of Rodents and Ferrets. As recent CPD events have 
illustrated, there has been a significant increase in interest and research 
into clinical and behavioural conditions affecting non-traditional animals 
generally, but also specifically guinea pigs. As a consequence of this we 
now have this BSAVA Manual of Guinea Pigs.
This new title, edited by the amazing team of John Chitty, Deborah 
Monks, Anna Meredith and Bob Doneley, awards guinea pigs a Manual 
of  their  own,  with  24  chapters  from  basic  biology  and  husbandry, 
through to diagnostic and clinical techniques, and a body system-based 
approach to clinical conditions. The all-new chapters come with the 
beautiful illustrations you would expect from a BSAVA Manual, including 
gorgeous  photos  and  fantastic  anatomical  drawings  by  LivingArt’s 
Samantha Elmhurst. The figures displaying advanced imaging modalities 
are especially high quality and are included for subjects such as dental 
and cardiorespiratory disease. Client handouts including a Guinea pig 
behaviour questionnaire and information on Feeding your guinea pigs 
a healthy diet are also included in print and available to download from 
the BSAVA library. Operative techniques for procedures including, for 
example, Extraction of the mandibular incisor, are found at the end of 
relevant chapters. There is a specific appendix on Investigating thin and 
anorexic guinea pigs alongside a guinea pig Formulary and a section 
on Toxic plants and substances. The book is appropriately referenced 
throughout.
Guinea pig owners rightly expect the standard of care of their pets to 
have improved in recent years and this book provides both general 
practice vets and advanced practitioners with a fantastic resource – one 
that should be on every practice bookshelf.
 
Liz Mullineaux BVM&S DVM&S CertSHP FRCVS 
RCVS Specialist in Wildlife Medicine (Mammalian)
